LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE AGE DISCRIMINATION CLAIM. PLAINTIFF'S DROP THE DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION CLAIM. THE COURT TAKES WAGE ISSUE CLAIMS AND PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM UNDER SUBMISSION. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312 AND CCP 437C(G) ON CD-DISC, MICROSOFT WORD FORMAT. JUDGE ERNEST H. LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF JOCELYN CRAWFORD-BROWN'S MOTION FOR DISMISSAL IS DENIED. MS. CRAWFORD-BROWN'S MOTION PAPERS FAIL TO EXPLAIN WHAT GROUNDS SHE OR HER COUNSEL HAD FOR HER TO FILE THIS LAWSUIT AS A CLASS ACTION. NOR DOES SHE EXPLAIN WHAT INDIVIDUAL DAMAGES SHE HAD WHICH SUPPORT THE SETTLEMENT AMOUNT. THE MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO REFILING OF THE MOTION. COUNSEL IS TO RE-NOTICE THE ONE HUNDRED PARTIES THEY SENT DISCOVERY NOTICES TO MAKING IT CLEAR THAT THEY ARE SEEKING TO KEEP THE SETTLEMENT CONFIDENTIAL. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR #5963. (302/HK)

Notice Of Renewed Motion And Renewed Motion For Dismissal
Off Calendar Jul-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Leonard Emma, Esq. (415-362-1111) of Employment Lawyers for Plaintiff.
John Ellis, Esq. (415-434-9100) of Sheppard Mullin Richter & Hampton LLP for Defendant/opposing party.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court asks a copy of the settlement agreement. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOCELYN CRAWFORD-BROWN Renewed Notice Of Motion And Renewed Motion For Dismissal is Granted. Order is signed.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The settlement agreement is returned to the party. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-26-2017 Continued To Jul-25-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: Appearance by Frank Zeccola, Esq. for Plaintiff. Appearance by John Ellis, Esq. for Defendant. The Order to Show Cause is re-set Jul-25-2017 at 10:30 AM in Dept. 610. JUDGE JAMES ROBERTSON; Clerk YMazariegos & JLee. Proceedings not reported. (610)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Document Under Seal
Off Calendar Dec-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Leonard Emma of Employment Lawters telephone number 415-362-1111 for Plaintiff Jocelyn Crawford-Brown.
John Ellis of Sheppard Mullin LLP telephone number 415-434-9100 for Defendant Dignity Health.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Jocelyn Crawford-Brown's motion to file her settlement agreement with defendant Dignity Health under seal is denied. The public, especially members of the putative class, have a very strong interest in learning about the terms of the settlement and the parties to the settlement have failed to explain why there private agreement of confidentiality trumps that interest. By failing to provide notice of the motion to dismiss the class claims to members of the putative class and seeking to shield the settlement agreement from public view, the parties have created the strong impression that they have something to hide from the putative class and the public. Counsel for Plaintiffs is to prepare a form of order and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Dismissal
Off Calendar Dec-15-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Leonard Emma of Employment Lawters telephone number 415-362-1111 for Plaintiff Jocelyn Crawford-Brown.
John Ellis of Sheppard Mullin LLP telephone number 415-434-9100 for Defendant Dignity Health.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Jocelyn Crawford-Brown's motion for dismissal is denied. Ms. Crawford-Brown's motion papers fail to explain what grounds she or her counsel had for her to file this lawsuit as a class action. Nor does she explain what individual damages she had which support the settlement amount. At page six of her memorandum Ms. Crawford-Brown correctly states the applicable case law that her motion should normally be granted provided that she did not receive a disproportionate recovery, yet she provides no facts on this point. The motion is denied without prejudice to refiling of the motion. Counsel is to re-notice the one hundred parties they sent discovery notices to making it clear that they are seeking to keep the settlement confidential. Counsel for Plaintiffs is to prepare a form of order and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963. (302/HK)

Michael Hoffman, Employment Lawyers, Tel. 415.362.1111, for plaintiffs;
Jonathan Barker, of Sheppard Mullin Richter & Hampton LLP, Tel. 213.620.1780, for defendants.

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Orders: Compelling Further Responses To Srogs And Rpds
Off Calendar Apr-27-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

Neither Party contested the tentative. Therefore, a hearing was not held and the Court adopted the tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JOCELYN CRAWFORD-BROWN, ON BEHALF OF HERSELF AND ALL OTHERS SIMILARLY SITUATED, AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC's Motion For Orders Compelling Responses To Special Interrogatories and Production Of Documents to Defendant Dignity Health is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. The motion is DENIED in regard to Special Interrogatory Nos. 18-21 and Request for Production Nos. 15 & 16 as overbroad and overly burdensome. The motion is GRANTED as to Request for Production Nos. 24-27.

Judge Pro Tem: Noah Lebowitz. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/JPT)
